meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Stop Abandoning Projects with AI Project Management
content.seo_description: Stop the cycle of unfinished apps. Learn how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to implement disciplined project management and finally ship your MVP.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of technical scoping and momentum. By integrating structured project management with AI-driven execution via Antigravity and Claude Code, you can decompose features into atomic, shippable units, ensuring your SaaS MVP moves from concept to production without stalling.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the codebase is a graveyard of half-baked features, half-implemented auth flows, and the crushing weight of 'almost done.' You have the vision, but your execution architecture is failing you. The gap between your laptop screen and a recurring revenue stream isn't a lack of coding skill—it's a lack of rigorous, systemized project management.

Today, we stop the bleeding. We are moving beyond the amateur 'just code until it works' mentality. We are implementing a high-velocity, AI-augmented workflow that treats your project like a professional product, not a hobby. It is time to stop building and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP Scope
steps.description: Strip your project down to the single most valuable feature. Use AI to prune scope bloat and define the 'must-haves' for your first revenue-generating version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Review my current project idea and extract the absolute minimum features required to launch a paid MVP. Create a prioritized roadmap that focuses on speed-to-market.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze my current project requirements and output a JSON roadmap of the 3 most critical features to ship this week.'

steps.title: Architect the Execution Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid file structure and database schema before writing logic. This prevents technical debt and future-proofs your SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a scalable folder structure and database schema for a SaaS MVP using [Tech Stack]. Ensure the architecture is modular for easy feature expansion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the core directory structure and Prisma schema file based on the architectural plan.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Scaffolding
steps.description: Stop writing boilerplate. Use AI to generate foundational code blocks, allowing you to focus on the unique business logic that drives value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the boilerplate for a secure user authentication flow and a dashboard skeleton. Include error handling and standard API response patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude generate 'Create the auth controller and dashboard layout components with Tailwind CSS.'

steps.title: Implement High-Velocity TDD
steps.description: Write tests alongside features. This ensures that as you add complexity, your MVP doesn't break, keeping your momentum high.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests for the authentication and primary feature flow. Use a TDD approach to validate logic before implementation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test 'Run the newly generated tests and fix any regression issues in the auth module.'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Loop
steps.description: Establish a continuous deployment pipeline so your code is always live. A live app is a motivation engine for a founder.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a CI/CD configuration file for [Deployment Platform] that automates testing and deployment on every git push.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the deployment pipeline and verify the production environment health check.'

pro_tips: Never start a coding session without a clear 3-bullet objective defined in a dedicated project.md file.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to scaffold, you are over-engineering; cut the scope immediately.
Use Claude Code as a pair programmer to review your own code for security vulnerabilities before every commit.
Track your 'shipping velocity' by counting features deployed per week, not lines of code written.
Kill any feature that doesn't directly contribute to either user acquisition or subscription conversion.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, gather feedback from 10 real users and iterate using the same AI-driven project management workflow to refine your core value proposition.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the framework to stop being a 'builder' and start being a 'founder.' Don't let your next project end up in the graveyard of unfinished ideas. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
